name: Cabezon (SS-334)
events: The history of USS Cabezon as compiled on this page is extracted from her patrol reports.
This page was last updated in March 2017.
14 Jan 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) arrived at Newport, Rhode Island from New London, Connecticut for torpedo trials.
15 Jan 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) departed Newport, Rhode Island for New London, Connecticut for repairs. Torpedo trials had to be cancelled/broken off.
24 Jan 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) arrived at Newport, Rhode Island from New London, Connecticut for torpedo trials.
29 Jan 1945 With her torpedo trials completed, USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN), returned to New London, Connecticut.
10 Feb 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises in Long Island Sound together with USS George W. Ingram (Lt.Cdr. A.S. Boyce, USNR).
11 Feb 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises in Long Island Sound together with USS George W. Ingram (Lt.Cdr. A.S. Boyce, USNR).
12 Feb 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises in Long Island Sound together with USS George W. Ingram (Lt.Cdr. A.S. Boyce, USNR).
13 Feb 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises in Long Island Sound together with USS George W. Ingram (Lt.Cdr. A.S. Boyce, USNR).
19 Feb 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) departed from New London, Connecticut bound for Key West, Florida.
25 Feb 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) arrived at Key West, Florida for services with the Fleet Sound School.
26 Feb 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises off Key West.
27 Feb 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises off Key West.
28 Feb 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises off Key West.
1 Mar 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises off Key West.
2 Mar 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises off Key West.
6 Mar 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises off Key West.
7 Mar 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises off Key West.
8 Mar 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises off Key West.
9 Mar 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises off Key West.
10 Mar 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises off Key West.
13 Mar 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises off Key West.
14 Mar 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises off Key West.
15 Mar 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises off Key West.
16 Mar 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) departed Key West, Florida for the Panama Canal Zone.
20 Mar 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) arrived at Coco Solo, Panama Canal Zone.

22 Mar 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises in Panama Bay together with USS Paul Jones (Lt.Cdr. H.M. Payne, USNR), USS Bulmer (Lt. T.C. Farrell, USNR) and USS MacLeish (Lt. D.S. Cramer, USNR).

23 Mar 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises in Panama Bay together with USS Paul Jones (Lt.Cdr. H.M. Payne, USNR), USS Bulmer (Lt. T.C. Farrell, USNR), USS MacLeish (Lt. D.S. Cramer, USNR) and USS USS SC-1299.

24 Mar 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises in Panama Bay together with USS Paul Jones (Lt.Cdr. H.M. Payne, USNR), USS Bulmer (Lt. T.C. Farrell, USNR), USS MacLeish (Lt. D.S. Cramer, USNR) and USS USS SC-1299.

25 Mar 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises in Panama Bay together with USS Paul Jones (Lt.Cdr. H.M. Payne, USNR), USS Bulmer (Lt. T.C. Farrell, USNR), USS MacLeish (Lt. D.S. Cramer, USNR), USS PC-1215, USS PC-1221 and USS SC-1299.

26 Mar 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises in Panama Bay together with USS Paul Jones (Lt.Cdr. H.M. Payne, USNR), USS MacLeish (Lt. D.S. Cramer, USNR), USS PC-1215, USS PC-1221 and USS SC-1299.
29 Mar 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) departed Balboa, Panama Canal Zone for Pearl Harbor.
15 Apr 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) arrived at Pearl Harbor.

25 Apr 1945 During 25 April 1945, USS Skate (T/Cdr. R.B. Lynch, USN) and USS Pipefish (T/Cdr. W.N. Deragon, USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor with USS Pruitt (Lt. D.B. Harby, USNR), USS J. Douglas Blackwood (Lt.Cdr. J.L. Johnston, USNR) and USS PC-599 (Lt. J.M Kindt, USNR).
They were then joined for night exercises by USS Cabezon (T/Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN), USS Capitaine (T/Lt.Cdr. E.S. Friedrick, USN) and USS Chew (Lt.Cdr. J.J. Mannion, USNR).

Night exercises were then carried out with a group of transports simulating a convoy. The group of transports was made up of the following vessels; USS Carlisle (Lt.Cdr. M.J. Carley, USNR), USS Cleburne (Lt.Cdr. F.T. Callahan, USNR), USS Crenshaw (Cdr. W.B. Studley, USNR), USS Crittenden (T/Capt. P.C. Crosley, USN), USS Cullman (Lt.Cdr. W.B. Dell, USNR), USS Riverside (T/Capt. L.N. Miller, USN), USS Bandera (T/Capt. A.C. Thorington, USN), USS Beckham (T/Capt. A.R. Sodergren, USN), USS Buckingham (T/Capt. H.G. Moran, USN), USS Kingsbury (T/Cdr. J.H. Hughes, USN), USS Lander (T/Capt. J.D. Sweeney, USN), USS Pickaway (T/Capt. H.L. Bixby, USN), USS Rockbridge (T/Capt. W.W. Cone, USN), USS San Saba (T/Capt. E.E. Berthold, USN), USS Waukesha (Lt.Cdr. J.S. Herold, USNR), USS Winston (Lt.Cdr. E.S. Doty, USNR) and USS Prentiss (T/Cdr. G.P. Walker, USN). [A source give that 18 transports were present so perhaps there was one more that is not listed here]. The convoy was escorted by 9 escorts according to the source which also stated that 18 transports had been present. USS Chew was the escort leader and he three escorts that had been exercising with the submarines also joined the convoy. Most of the above transports had already been out for exercises for several days during which they had been escorted by USS PC-486 (Lt. R.B. Whitney, USNR), USS PC-781 (Lt. F. Hopkinson, USNR) and USS PC-1078 (Lt. J.R. Slater, USNR) which were still present. [That leaves two escort vessels still unaccounted for.]
The exercises were completed on the 26th.
6 May 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor together with USS Trever (Lt. A.S. Brengle, USNR) and USS Thornback (Cdr. E.P. Abrahamson, USN).

7 May 1945 During 7/8 May 1945, USS Lapon (T/Lt.Cdr. D.G. Baer, USN), USS Skate (T/Cdr. R.B. Lynch, USN), USS Cabezon (T/Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) and USS Loggerhead (Cdr. R.N. Metcalf, USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor together with USS Pruitt (Lt. D.B. Harby, USNR) and USS Coolbaugh (Lt.Cdr. S.T. Hotchkiss, USNR). These included night exercises.

9 May 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor together with USS Whitman (Lt. E.A. Sandor, USNR), USS Florikan (Lt. D. Hima, USN) and USS Loggerhead (Cdr. R.N. Metcalf, USN).
10 May 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor together with USS Whitman (Lt. E.A. Sandor, USNR) and USS Le Hardy (Lt.Cdr. J.W. Dayton, Jr., USNR).
25 May 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 1st war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Sea of Okhotsk.
For daily and attack positions of USS Cabezon during this patrol see the map below.
29 May 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) arrived at Midway to top off with fuel and some minor voyage repairs.
30 May 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) departed Midway for her patrol area.

19 Jun 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) torpedoed and sank the Japanese merchant cargo ship Zaosan Maru (2631 GRT, built 1906) south-west of Paramushiro, Kuril Islands in position 50°39'N, 154°38'E.
(All times are zone K, -10) 18 July 1945 2140 hours - Received a contact report from USS Apogon.
2150 hours - Went ahead full to intercept.
2240 hours - Received a contact report from USS Dace. This appears to be a different contact.
2325 hours - Received a message from USS Apogon that she is going to attack.
2333 hours - Apogon reported her attack completed.
19 July 1945 0005 hours - Apogon reported two ships sunk, possibly three.
0040 hours - Received another position report from Apogon.
0115 hours - Radar contact on USS Apogon.
0125 hours - Radar contact on the target bearing 170°(T), range 15000 yards. Commenced tracking and approach.
0200 hours - Targets is zig-zagging on a base course 350°(T), speed 9.5 knots.
0215 hours - In position 50°39'N, 154°38'E fired three torpedoes from 2250 yards.
0218 hours - Observed two timed hits and possibly a third hit. The target exploded and disappeared from the radar screen shortly afterwards.
11 Jul 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) ended her 1st war patrol at Midway.
4 Aug 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) departed Midway bound for Saipan.
13 Aug 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) arrived at Saipan from Midway.
31 Aug 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) departed Saipan for Subic Bay, Philippines.
7 Sep 1945 USS Cabezon (Cdr. G.W. Lautrup, USN) arrived at Subic Bay.
